title: neogaillonia tinctoria
intro: Neogaillonia tinctoria, also known as Dyer's Woad, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Neogaillonia tinctoria include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe skin irritations. Neogaillonia tinctoria is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
